title: TileMaxSim: IO-Aware GPU MaxSim Scoring with Dimension Tiling and Fused Product Quantization
abstract: arXiv:2606.26439v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Multi-vector retrieval models such as ColBERT achieve state-of-the-art accuracy through fine-grained token-level MaxSim scoring, yet existing GPU implementations leave most hardware performance unused. We give a roofline analysis of MaxSim on modern GPUs and identify a severe bandwidth gap: naive implementations reach only 5-18% of peak HBM bandwidth because they materialize the Nq x Nd similarity matrix, wasting memory traffic on data that is consumed once and discarded. We present TileMaxSim, a family of IO-aware Triton kernels that close this gap via (1) multi-query SRAM tiling that streams document embeddings through shared memory while accumulating per-query-token maxima in registers, reading each embedding from HBM exactly once; (2) dimension tiling that partitions the embedding dimension into 128-wide chunks, enabling scoring for d > 128 embeddings that overflow shared memory; and (3) fused product-quantization scoring via shared-

Title: TileMaxSim: IO-Aware GPU MaxSim Scoring with Dimension Tiling and Fused Product Quantization
Abstract: arXiv:2606.26439v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Multi-vector retrieval models such as ColBERT achieve state-of-the-art accuracy through fine-grained token-level MaxSim scoring, yet existing GPU implementations leave most hardware performance unused. We give a roofline analysis of MaxSim on modern GPUs and identify a severe bandwidth gap: naive implementations reach only 5-18% of peak HBM bandwidth because they materialize the Nq x Nd similarity matrix, wasting memory traffic on data that is consumed once and discarded. We present TileMaxSim, a family of IO-aware Triton kernels that close this gap via (1) multi-query SRAM tiling that streams document embeddings through shared memory while accumulating per-query-token maxima in registers, reading each embedding from HBM exactly once; (2) dimension tiling that partitions the embedding dimension into 128-wide chunks, enabling scoring for d > 128 embeddings that overflow shared memory; and (3) fused product-quantization scoring via shared-
translated_title: TileMaxSim：通过维度分块和融合乘积量化实现IO感知的GPU MaxSim评分
translated_abstract: 多向量检索模型（如ColBERT）通过细粒度的token级MaxSim评分达到了最先进的准确率，但现有的GPU实现并未充分利用大部分硬件性能。我们对现代GPU上的MaxSim进行了屋顶线分析，发现了一个严重的带宽瓶颈：朴素实现仅能达到峰值HBM带宽的5-18%，因为它们会具体化Nq×Nd相似度矩阵，浪费了内存流量在那些仅被使用一次后即丢弃的数据上。我们提出了TileMaxSim，这是一系列IO感知的Triton内核，通过以下方法弥合了这一差距：(1) 多查询SRAM分块，在共享内存中流式传输文档嵌入，同时在寄存器中累积每个查询token的最大值，从HBM中恰好读取每个嵌入一次；(2) 维度分块，将嵌入维度划分为128宽的块，使得评分可以处理因共享内存溢出而无法容纳的d>128嵌入；(3) 通过共享的融合乘积量化评分。
tldr: TileMaxSim通过多查询SRAM分块、维度分块和融合乘积量化三种IO感知优化策略，解决了GPU上MaxSim评分的带宽瓶颈问题，使HBM带宽利用率从5-18%大幅提升。
en_tdlr: TileMaxSim addresses the bandwidth bottleneck of MaxSim scoring on GPUs through three IO-aware optimization strategies—multi-query SRAM tiling, dimension tiling, and fused product quantization—boosting HBM bandwidth utilization from 5-18% to significantly higher levels.
